conbody.p: Pumpkins are one of the most recognizable symbols of Halloween, commonly associated with seasonal decoration, costume imagery, and the tradition of carving jack-o’-lanterns. Their connection to Halloween combines agricultural history, folklore, and modern popular culture.

section.title: Historical Origins
section.p: Pumpkins are native to North America and have long been cultivated as a food source. Their association with Halloween can be traced to European folklore, particularly stories involving carved lanterns used to ward off evil spirits. When these traditions migrated to North America, pumpkins became a practical substitute for other root vegetables due to their size and availability.

section.title: Symbolic Meaning
section.p: As a symbol, pumpkins often represent the transition from harvest season into winter. In the context of Halloween, they are commonly associated with protection, transformation, and the boundary between the ordinary world and the supernatural.
The carved jack-o’-lantern, in particular, is believed to symbolize guidance and vigilance, using light to keep harmful forces at bay.

section.title: Modern Halloween Usage
section.p: Today, pumpkins are widely used as decorative elements during Halloween. They appear in carved displays, artificial decorations, printed media, and digital imagery. Pumpkin carving has also become a social and creative activity, often involving families or community events.

example.title: Common Uses of Pumpkins During Halloween
